title: When You Don't Know Anyone Yet
summary: Social baseline loneliness after a move is common — small routines that help on day one.

sections.title: The Moment
sections.value: Evening of day one. The group chat is quiet. You realise you have not spoken out loud to anyone since the Uber driver.

sections.title: The Feeling
sections.value: Not crisis — more like a room without furniture. You are between old ties and new ones, and the gap feels personal.

sections.title: The Reveal
sections.value: Relocation loneliness is extremely common. It does not mean you chose wrong. It means you are building a social world from zero.

sections.title: How It Actually Works
sections.value: Aim for one small real-world interaction tomorrow. Message one person you trust with a specific time to call. Take a ten-minute walk outside. Avoid comparing your day one to other people's highlight posts.

sections.title: The Bridge
sections.value: When you are ready, find parks, community spaces and low-key venues near you.

sections.title: Next Chapter
sections.value: Tomorrow, open Week 1 — cooking, utilities, and the rest of settling in.
